subject: Cloud Computing
title: Cloud Repatriation: Coming Home
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Cloud Repatriation: Coming Home.

hook.text: 37signals moved off the cloud and saved $7 million over five years. Is leaving the cloud the new trend?

buildup.text: At scale, cloud can be more expensive than owned hardware. Some companies are moving workloads back on-premises.

discovery.text: Repatriation makes sense for stable, predictable workloads. Bursty, unpredictable work still belongs in the cloud.

twist.text: Most companies aren't going all-in or all-out. They're finding the right mix: cloud for some things, owned for others.

climax.text: The debate isn't cloud versus on-premises. It's which workloads belong where. The answer is different for every company.

punchline.text: Some left the cloud. Most stay. The right answer varies.

quiz.question: When does cloud repatriation make financial sense?
options: For stable, predictable workloads where owned hardware costs less at scale
It always makes sense to leave the cloud immediately
Only when a company has fewer than 10 employees
